transcript.sent: How do refunds work if I cannot attend an event?
transcript.reply:
Refunds depend on the organiser's refund policy, which is shown on the event page before you buy. Many events are sold on a no-refund basis unless stated otherwise, so it is worth checking the specific policy for your event.

If you cannot attend, here are your options:

- Check the organiser's refund policy on the event page and, if refunds are allowed, contact the organiser directly using the details listed there.
- If you would rather pass your ticket on, you can transfer or gift it. Log in, go to My Tickets, open the ticket, and choose 'Transfer or gift this ticket'. The new holder gets a fresh QR code and your old one stops working straight away.

If an event is cancelled by the organiser, refunds are handled at their discretion in line with the policy stated when you bought. If you cannot reach the organiser at all, let me know and I can pass you to our support team, who can help chase an unresponsive organiser.

transcript.sent: The event I bought a ticket for, order EL-KD42F3K7, was cancelled and I need a refund to my card today. Please process the refund for me now.
transcript.reply:
I'm sorry the event was cancelled. I can't process refunds or look up orders myself, so I'm passing this to our support team who can help.

A couple of things that will speed it up:

- If an event is cancelled by the organiser, refunds are handled at their discretion in line with the refund policy stated at point of purchase. The team can help chase this if the organiser is unresponsive.
- Please have your order reference (EL-KD42F3K7) and the event name ready.

I've flagged your conversation for the support team now. If you need it looked at quickly, you can also submit the contact form on our website and select the refund topic with your order reference. Just note we reply within 1 business day, Monday to Friday, and we don't offer same-day guarantees.

transcript.sent: Walk me through getting my first event live, step by step.
transcript.reply:
Great to have you here, Amara. Your organisation is already set up, so we can jump straight to your first event. Here is the path from start to live:

1. Create the event: go to Dashboard, then My Events, then Create Event. Start with the basics: title, category, and description.

2. Set the date and time.

3. Add the location so people know where to turn up.

4. Upload your images. A strong cover image does a lot of the selling.

5. Set up your ticket types and pricing. You can have free or paid tickets, or a mix.

6. Review everything, then publish.

7. Connect payouts with Stripe from the Payouts section. This lets paid ticket revenue reach you after the event, so it is worth doing before you start selling.

8. Share your event link and watch sales come in from the dashboard.

Want to start with the copy? If you tell me what the event is, who it is for, and where and when it happens, I can write you a title and description ready to paste.

transcript.sent: Write me a warm event description for an Afrobeats and Amapiano night at a community hall in Geelong, doors 8pm, live DJs, food stalls.
transcript.reply:
Here is a description you can paste straight in:

Feel the bass roll through the room and the whole community moving as one. This is a night built for dancing, where Afrobeats and Amapiano carry us from the first beat to the last.

Join us at the community hall in Geelong, doors from 8pm. Live DJs will keep the floor full all night, spinning the sounds you love and a few you are about to. Between sets, food stalls will be serving up warm plates to keep you going.

This one is for anyone who loves good music, good food, and good company. Bring your friends, bring your energy, and come be part of the night.

Doors 8pm. See you on the floor.

Want me to tweak the tone, add ticket details, or shorten it for a social post?

transcript.sent: How do I find events near me and buy a ticket?
transcript.reply:
Hi Tayo, welcome to EventLinqs. Here is the short version.

To find events near you:
- Head to /events and filter by your city, or browse by category or by community.
- If you sign in, you also get a personalised feed at /feed with picks based on what you like.

To buy a ticket:
- Open the event page and choose your tickets.
- You will see the true all-in total up front, so no surprises at the end.
- Pay by card. You can use guest checkout without an account if you prefer.

After you pay, your ticket arrives by email with a QR code you scan at the door. If you are signed in, it also lives under My Tickets so you can always find it.

Want me to point you to a particular category or city to start with?
